title: Measles Cases and Outbreaks | Measles (Rubeola) | CDC

number of measles cases reported among international visitors to the United States. Cases are assigned to a year based on the epidemiological week of the date of rash onset. For example, cases with a rash onset date occurring during the last epidemiological week of 2025 (December 28, 2025, through January 3, 2026) are included in the 2025 case counts. 
Demographic and clinical data using the national total. 
The number of confirmed measles cases among U.S. residents in each jurisdiction. 
The cumulative number of measles outbreaks (defined as 3 or more related cases) that have occurred this year in the United States. 
Why do state case counts on this webpage differ from the numbers posted on state health department websites? 
State jurisdictions and CDC publicly report measles cases on their respective websites on different schedules. CDC is aware of probable measles cases being reported by jurisdictions, but updates on this webpage reflect only confirmed measles cases notified to CDC as of noon on Thursdays. States may report three or more linked cases in their state as an outbreak, but CDC may not report it as a distinct outbreak if these cases are associated with a previously reported outbreak.
Why is there more measles activity?
